notes: Largest EU battery market. NMC banned for new commercial installations from 2025-01 due to carbon footprint thresholds. FCR/aFRR markets fully open. V2G pilot under §14a EnWG.
Hydropower dominant (low CO2 grid). NMC restrictions follow DE pattern. Strong incentive for self-consumption via EAG 2021. Solid-state pilots not yet approved.
Non-EU but follows EU regs voluntarily. Lower CO2 grid due to hydro+nuclear. Slightly relaxed thresholds. V2G regulation still in consultation.
V2G most mature market in EU (Utrecht pilots). High feed-in tariff post-2024 reform. Frequency market open via TenneT NL.
Three-region complexity (Flanders/Wallonia/Brussels). Lower nuclear share post-2025 will raise grid CO2. Modest battery adoption.
Small market but high GDP/capita. Most electricity imported from FR/DE/BE (low local CO2 attribution). Battery uptake niche but premium.
World leader in wind. Frequency market via Energinet most volatile in EU = highest FCR revenues. Strong V2G grid code already drafted.
Lowest grid CO2 in Europe (hydro+nuclear). Self-consumption ROI weaker but capacity market revenue strong. Cold climate favors LFP+Sodium-Ion.
Cold-climate stress tests batteries — thermal management required for NMC/NCA. Sodium-Ion ideal candidate. Fingrid runs FCR-N market.
Non-EU (EEA). Hydropower 96% — grid CO2 near zero. Statnett FCR market lucrative. V2G mature — Oslo trial fleets active.
Non-EU — relaxed thresholds. NMC still common. Battery market growing but regulation lags. EXIST day-ahead market provides TOU signal.
Post-Brexit divergence — slightly relaxed than EU 2023/1542 (UK BR 2024). Capacity Market (T-4/T-1 auctions) major revenue. National Grid ESO Dynamic Containment market for frequency.
